summary: Large decompilation pass for src/melee/ty/tyfigupon.c, covering Toy Figure Pon menu setup, cameras, panel/coin/lever assets, bet/count displays, trophy unlock flow, cleanup, and several GObj process callbacks. The PR also tightened related headers and structs, especially ToyAnimState and un_804D6EF4_t, and adjusted local data symbol scope. decomp.dev reported +1804 matched bytes, 10 new matches overall, and many tyfigupon functions improving from 0% to high partial or 100% match.

key_files.role: Main decompilation target; replaced many placeholder functions with C, added local asset strings, camera descriptor, local structs, and matching tactics.
Updated prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to typed signatures for the newly decompiled tyfigupon functions.
Expanded struct un_804D6EF4_t with typed fields including HSD_GObj*, HSD_JObj* jobjs[16], archive pointer, shorts, and state bytes.
Refined ToyAnimState layout, including size 0x14 note, xC, x0E-x11 state bytes, and padding.
Converted un_804A2AA8 usage from raw byte/pointer arithmetic to ToyAnimState fields.
Changed memzero of un_804A2AA8 to use &un_804A2AA8 and sizeof(ToyAnimState).
Included ty/types.h and changed extern un_804A2AA8 from u8[] to ToyAnimState.
Corrected un_803087F4 return type from void to HSD_GObj*.
Changed several tyfigupon .data symbols from global to local scope.

what_changed: Implemented or substantially decompiled many tyfigupon functions: fn_803152BC, un_803153EC, fn_803155C8, fn_80315C44, fn_80316170, fn_8031638C, un_80316420, fn_803168DC, fn_80316C24, un_8031753C, un_80317A60, un_80317D80_OnEnter, and un_803181BC.

Refined existing tyfigupon code: tyFigupon_80314C5C was brought close to matching with stack padding, translation accessor calls, post-decrement behavior, explicit 0x58 allocation, ABS-based float math, and jobjs indexing; tyFigupon_80316BF8 now takes an HSD_GObj* parameter.

Added tyfigupon asset string literals for ToyFigurePon panel, background, coin, number, bet, lever, parameter, and nget joints plus their anim/matanim/shapeanim names, language-specific archives TyMnFigp.dat/TyMnFigp.usd and SdToy.dat/SdToy.usd, and error/report strings.
Added a static HSD_CameraDescPerspective tyfigupon_cam_desc and setup code for camera GObjs, GX links, text objects, language-specific SisLib data, and camera tilt via substick input.

Added logic for Toy Figure Pon panel construction, coin spawning, bet count display, probability display, lever/animation state machine, trophy unlock through Trophy_SetUnlockState, and cleanup via un_803181BC.
Promoted un_804A2AA8 to a typed ToyAnimState global shared by toy.c and tylist.h instead of treating it as a u8 buffer.
Expanded un_804D6EF4_t so tyfigupon code can use jobjs[0..0xE], archive, x54/x56/x58, and x5C/x5D/x5E instead of opaque padding.
Updated tyfigupon.h prototypes to concrete argument and return types, including HSD_GObj* callbacks, s16 arguments, void-return helpers, and s32-return query/cleanup functions.
Changed config/GALE01/symbols.txt entries un_803FEA10, un_803FEB60, un_803FEED4, and un_803FEEE8 from global to local scope.
smart_moves: Typed the shared un_804A2AA8 state as ToyAnimState before updating callers, which removed brittle byte-offset access and made jobj[0]/jobj[1] intent searchable across toy.c and tylist.
Represented the contiguous JObj region in un_804D6EF4_t as jobjs[16], enabling indexed access such as jobjs[0xE] instead of a collection of fake individual pointer fields.
Kept uncertain semantic names as xNN/pad_NN while still recording exact offsets and types, avoiding overnaming while preserving match-critical structure layout.
Used local static string variables for archive/public-symbol names, which helps reproduce .data ordering and makes the asset dependencies explicit.
Chose explicit HSD_MemAlloc(0x58) where the current Toy struct size did not match the runtime allocation, and left a TODO noting that Toy should only be 0x58.
Carried exact engine API usage into the decomp: HSD_ArchiveGetPublicAddress, HSD_JObjLoadJoint, HSD_JObjAddChild, GObj_SetupGXLink, HSD_GObjProc_8038FD54, HSD_SisLib calls, and lbArchive_LoadSymbols.
Documented remaining matching pain directly in comments, including regswap issues and permuter slop, instead of hiding non-final matching work.
decomp_lessons: When an apparent byte blob is shared by multiple files, promote it into a real shared struct in types.h and update externs/callers together.
Consecutive pointer fields used as a table should often become an array; here jobjs[16] made tyfigupon code much cleaner and more matchable.
Do not force a wrong struct size to satisfy allocation sites. This PR used explicit 0x58 allocations and left a size TODO for Toy rather than pretending the existing struct was correct.
For HSD text objects, prefer real HSD_Text fields such as hidden over local fake overlays when the type evidence supports it.
Large menu decomp often needs asset-name reconstruction, language branches, SisLib setup, camera descriptors, GObj process scheduling, and cleanup paths all in the same pass.

Partial decomp with high match percentages is still useful when prototypes, data layout, and struct typing unlock future work; the bot reported several tyfigupon functions at 87-99% even where not yet 100%.
naming_conventions: File-scoped known functions use the existing tyFigupon_ prefix, while unidentified functions retain fn_803xxxxx or un_803xxxxx names.
Unknown struct fields remain offset-style xNN with pad_NN arrays and offset comments, e.g. x54, x58, x5C, pad_0C, pad_12.
Local asset strings use descriptive str_* names based on the exact public symbol or file name, such as str_panel_joint, str_archive_us, str_coin_matanim, and str_sdtoy_data_us.
The shared toy animation state is named ToyAnimState and exposes jobj[2] for the two alternating JObjs.
The broader tyfigupon runtime structs are still provisional names, TyFiguponData, TyFiguponUD, and TyFiguponED4, reflecting limited semantic certainty.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used #pragma dont_inline around un_80314B54 to preserve expected call structure.
Inserted PAD_STACK with exact sizes such as 40, 32, 24, 16, and 8 to shape stack frames.
Added comments identifying known register-allocation problems: a 3-way r29/r30/r31 regswap in tyFigupon_80314C5C and remaining regswap/permuter issues in other functions.
Changed --tp1->x8 to tp1->x8-- in tyFigupon_80314C5C, indicating decrement ordering mattered for the match.
Used HSD_JObjGetTranslationX/Y/Z accessors instead of direct struct field reads in places where that matched generated code.
Used ABS macro and carefully reused float temporaries to reproduce float math and register lifetimes.
Introduced small static const data such as Vec3 un_803B8968 and DigitInit un_803B8974 to match constant/data placement.
Used explicit casts and signedness controls, including s8 state fields, s16 counts, u32 divisions of gm_801623D8()/10u, and f32 probability calculations.
The author explicitly left a screenshot/comment for future permuter work, saying it was for someone with more patience with permuter.
review_feedback: No formal GitHub review comments or reviews were present in the slice.
decomp.dev reported GALE01 matched code improved by +0.05% and +1804 bytes.
decomp.dev listed 10 new matches overall; tyfigupon items reaching 100% included fn_803152BC, fn_8031638C, and un_803181BC.

decomp.dev listed tyfigupon unmatched-item improvements including fn_80316C24 to 95.56%, fn_803155C8 to 99.42%, un_8031753C to 94.55%, fn_80315C44 to 89.67%, un_80316420 to 97.55%, un_80317D80_OnEnter to 93.70%, un_80317A60 to 99.97%, fn_803168DC to 87.42%, fn_80316170 to 99.36%, and un_803153EC to 87.38%.

The only human follow-up text was the author posting an image and saying: Leaving it here for someone who has more patience with permuter than me :). This is useful evidence of remaining permuter/matching cleanup but not a reviewer request.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that resolve the Toy struct size TODO and make STATIC_ASSERT(sizeof(struct Toy) == 0x58) viable.
Search for subsequent renames of TyFiguponData, TyFiguponUD, un_804D6EF4_t fields x54/x58/x5C/x5D/x5E, and jobjs indices.
Look for future tyfigupon permuter cleanup on fn_803155C8, fn_80315C44, fn_80316C24, un_8031753C, un_80317D80_OnEnter, and un_803153EC.
Check whether the un_803087F4 return type change to HSD_GObj* was reused in other toy or trophy-display decompilation work.
Search config symbol history for un_803FEA10, un_803FEB60, un_803FEED4, and un_803FEEE8 to see whether later names replaced the local un_* data symbols.
